body.title: Add source_agent to memories; Librarian write endpoint for all agents
body.result: Superseded by T-memory-write-path — fuller scope: tag extraction at write time, Scraps embedding, typed payloads, source_agent. Decision D-recall-api-2026-05-28.
body.description:
Any agent should be able to deposit a memory without going through Igor's cognition pipeline. Currently the write path requires Igor to be running (consolidation, dreaming). Add a source_agent field to clan.memories so records carry provenance. Expose a write endpoint on the Librarian (MCP tool: memory_write or similar) that any agent can call directly. Igor's cognition continues writing through the same endpoint — it just becomes one writer among many rather than the only writer.

**Affected files:** devices/librarian/ (new MCP tool), clan.memories schema (migration for source_agent column), devices/igor/cognition/consolidation paths (update to call Librarian write API rather than DB directly — or keep direct write and just add the column)
**Design rules:** no-sqlite, igor-uses-not-contains
**Scope boundary:** IN — source_agent column + Librarian write tool; OUT — do not change retrieval logic or interpretive_edges in this ticket
**Test plan:** CC writes a memory via mcp__librarian__memory_write; query returns it with correct source_agent; Igor-written memories retain source_agent='Igor-wild-0001'
